LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS STEPHEN LEE AND GOLDEN PROSPERITIES MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION TO CONTEST THE INSURERS APPLICATION FOR GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT DETERMINATION IS GRANTED. THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT IS NOT WITHIN THE BALLPARK. (SEE TECH-BILT, INC. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOCIATES (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488, 499.) THE PARTY ASSERTING THE LACK OF GOOD FAITH HAS THE BURDEN OF PROOF ON THAT ISSUE. (CCP SEC. 877.6, SUBD. (D).) HOWEVER, AFTER THE NON-SETTLING DEFENDANTS ATTACKED THE SETTLEMENT AS LACKING IN GOOD FAITH, THE SETTLING DEFENDANTS WERE REQUIRED TO FILE COUNTERAFFIDAVITS (CCP SEC. 877.6, SUBD. (B)) TO MAKE AN EVIDENTIARY SHOWING THAT THE SETTLEMENT WAS "IN THE BALLPARK." (MATTCO FORGE, INC. V. ARTHUR YOUNG & CO. (1995) 38 CAL.APP.4TH 1137, FN. 6.) THE EVIDENCE, TAKEN AS A WHOLE, INDICATES THAT THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT IS NOT WITHIN THE BALLPARK BECAUSE THE AMOUNT DOES NOT ACCOUNT FOR PLAINTIFF'S POTENTIAL RECOVERY FOR GENERAL DAMAGES. ADDITIONALLY, THE EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT NOT ALL OF THE DEFENDANTS IDENTIFIED IN THE AGREEMENT, AS RELEASED PARTIES, CONSENTED TO THE AGREEMENT. (SEE DECLARATION OF SHARON SETO.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Cross-Defendant Marthe Schreiber'S Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Continued To Oct-31-16 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 502
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseoh Brent, Esq. and R. Wesley Pratt, Esq. for specially appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant/Responding party Stephen K. Lee.

The Court reviews the proposed Judgment on the Verdict forms submitted by Mr. Brent Mr. Hagen notes his concerns. The Court determines that it will sign the form of Judgment which includes the case citations, signing the Judgment in open court. The clerk leaves the Courtroom to prepare and file copies of the Judgment.

The Court and counsel next address the Cross-defendant's Motion to Strike the Cross-Complaint. The Court notes certain procedural concerns not limited to whether the matter is moot, whether the Court has standing or jurisdiction to consider the motion, and whether the effect of its ruling either way would occasion grounds for immediate appeal, staying the proceeding in its entirety.

Counsel exit the Courtroom to discuss the matter out of the presence of the Court. Upon their return, Mr. Brent advises the Court that counsel have stipulated that the Court may defer ruling on the motion until all post-trial motions have been heard. The Court so holds. Mr. Brent will prepare and circulate a form of Order.

Subsequent to hearing, the Court determines to continue the matter for 90 days pending submission by counsel of a stipulated Order concerning the status of the motion.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Wesley Pratt and Joseph Brent of Brent, Fiol & Pratt LLP telephone number 415-259-4515 for Plaintiff Marthe Schreiber.
Alison Crane of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane LLP telephone number 415-981-5411 for Defendant Stephen Lee.
Fredrick Hagen of Berding & Weil LLP telephone number 925-838-2090 for Defendant Golden Propserities Management Company.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tenative ruling.
DEFENDANTS STEPHEN LEE and GOLDEN PROSPERITIES MANAGEMENT COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. Although the notice of motion states that the motion was filed by both Mr. Lee and Golden Prosperities and the reply brief makes arguments on behalf of Golden Prosperities, the separate statement filed in support of the motion was filed only by Mr. Lee and all three issues stated therein only relate to Mr. Lee and all arguments made in the initial memorandum were made only on behalf of Mr. Lee. Thus, the court treats the motion as only filed by Mr. Lee and does not address the arguments in the reply brief made on behalf of Golden Prosperities. There are ample facts showing that there are material factual disputes precluding summary judgment in favor of Mr. Lee on the sole claim alleged against him as well as precluding summary adjudication in his favor on the request for punitive damages against him. Specifically, there are triable issues whether: 1) Mr. Lee was in control of the property at the time of the accident; and 2) at the time Mr. Lee constructed the skylight and by his failure to correct the asserted unsafe skylight, Mr. Lee acted with conscious disregard of the safety of persons (including Ms. Schreiber) who came near the skylight. (Ms. Schreiber's Facts 1-13, 26- 44, and 95-124). Order signed in open Court. The parties are to meet and confer, and try and resolve other remaining issues.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest The Insurers Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Helen Chen of Robinson & Wood, Inc. telephone number 408-298-7120 for Defendants Peter Lee, Eva Wong, Anne Lee and Stephen K. Lee Enterprises.
Fredrick Hagen of Berding & Weil LLP telephone number 925-838-2090 for Defendants Stephen K. Lee, Golden Prosperities Management and specially appearing for Sharon Szeto.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS STEPHEN LEE AND GOLDEN PROSPERITIES MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LLC'S Motion To Contest The Insurers Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination is granted. The settlement amount is not within the ballpark. (See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) The party asserting the lack of good faith has the burden of proof on that issue. (CCP Sec. 877.6, subd. (d).) However, after the non-settling defendants attacked the settlement as lacking in good faith, the settling defendants were required to file counteraffidavits (CCP Sec. 877.6, subd. (b)) to make an evidentiary showing that the settlement was "in the ballpark." (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1137, fn. 6.) The evidence, taken as a whole, indicates that the settlement amount is not within the ballpark because the amount does not account for Plaintiff's potential recovery for general damages. Additionally, the evidence indicates that not all of the defendants identified in the agreement, as released parties, consented to the agreement. (See Declaration of Sharon Seto.) Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
